pubmed_links: [Shen et al. (2012) Genetic and functional analysis of the gene encoding GAP-43 in schizophrenia](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/22138049/); [Tian et al. (2007) Decreased GAP-43 immunoreactivity in post mortem hippocampus of bipolar disorder and schizophrenia](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/17095155/); [Noda et al. (2024) An unstable variant of GAP43 leads to neurodevelopmental deficiency](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39738362/)
summary: GAP43 encodes a presynaptic phosphoprotein critical for axonal growth and synaptic plasticity; altered GAP43 expression has been repeatedly reported in postmortem brains of individuals with schizophrenia and bipolar disorder, and heterozygous knockout mice display autistic-like social and behavioral deficits, with rare de novo variants linked to neurodevelopmental disorders including intellectual disability. (emerging evidence)
